channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: SoFi Stadium workers vote to authorize strike ahead of World Cup
item.description: Negotiations between the union representing the workers, the hospitality group at the Los Angeles stadium and FIFA are set to continue Monday.
item.content:encoded:
Negotiations between the union representing the workers, the hospitality group at the Los Angeles stadium and FIFA are set to continue Monday.

(Image credit: Jae C. Hong)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Why one historian uses social media to remember D-Day in real time
item.description: At the National World War II Memorial, historian Alex Kershaw has found an unlikely way to keep D-Day alive: live social media posts timed to the events of June 6, 1944.
item.content:encoded: At the National World War II Memorial, historian Alex Kershaw has found an unlikely way to keep D-Day alive: live social media posts timed to the events of June 6, 1944.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: From Ragtime to Schmigadoon: What to look for at the Tony Awards
item.description: It was a great year for plays, but a so-so year for musicals. NPR's Jeff Lunden points out what to look for at the Tony Awards this year.
item.content:encoded: It was a great year for plays, but a so-so year for musicals. NPR's Jeff Lunden points out what to look for at the Tony Awards this year.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Despite a competitive market, finding a summer job is highly beneficial for teens
item.description: Teenagers looking for summer jobs face a tough labor market. But the personal benefits are huge.
item.content:encoded:
Teenagers looking for summer jobs face a tough labor market. But the personal benefits are huge.

(Image credit: Etienne Laurent)
item.dc:creator: Dianna Douglas

item.title: Visiting a beautiful oasis in Phoenix's Rio Salado Habitat Restoration Area
item.description: Tucked away in hot, dry, concrete and asphalt sprawl of Phoenix is a small sliver of the Salt River's green, damp natural beauty.
item.content:encoded: Tucked away in hot, dry, concrete and asphalt sprawl of Phoenix is a small sliver of the Salt River's green, damp natural beauty.
item.dc:creator: Alex Hager

item.title: Trump travels to Wisconsin for an agriculture roundtable
item.description: President Trump headed to Wisconsin hoping to reassure farmers his agenda is working for them despite high gas prices and other hurdles thanks to the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: President Trump headed to Wisconsin hoping to reassure farmers his agenda is working for them despite high gas prices and other hurdles thanks to the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Independent gas station owners are feeling a severe financial squeeze
item.description: As the Iran war continues, many independent gas stations in the U.S. are struggling to deal with volatile fuel prices. NPR's Eyder Peralta speaks with Jivtesh Gill, who owns several stations in California.
item.content:encoded: As the Iran war continues, many independent gas stations in the U.S. are struggling to deal with volatile fuel prices. NPR's Eyder Peralta speaks with Jivtesh Gill, who owns several stations in California.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: Experts David Michener and Doug Conley discuss University of Michigan's peony garden
item.description: The peonies are in peak bloom at the University of Michigan's W.E. Upjohn Peony Garden. NPR's Eyder Perala speaks with the garden's curator, David Michener, and horticulturist, Doug Conley.
item.content:encoded: The peonies are in peak bloom at the University of Michigan's W.E. Upjohn Peony Garden. NPR's Eyder Perala speaks with the garden's curator, David Michener, and horticulturist, Doug Conley.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: As American elections become more tense, officials are turning to local police
item.description: Since the 2020 election, local law enforcement has increasingly been playing a bigger role in helping local officials secure elections.
item.content:encoded:
Since the 2020 election, local law enforcement has increasingly been playing a bigger role in helping local officials secure elections.

(Image credit: Ronda Churchill)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: Deported Filipino sailors say they were falsely linked to child sexual abuse material
item.description: NPR has tracked deported Filipino sailors who say they were accused without evidence of possessing child sexual exploitation material. Almost none have been charged or prosecuted.
item.content:encoded: NPR has tracked deported Filipino sailors who say they were accused without evidence of possessing child sexual exploitation material. Almost none have been charged or prosecuted.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán
